title: Skill Discovery, Trigger Descriptions, and Boundaries · Protocol Clinic: Explicit Invocation
summary: Make explicit and implicit invocation reliable without false positives. Planned artifact: Add and verify the agent ext explicit invocation increment in a trigger evaluation dataset. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Support explicit invocation when deterministic use is required.
Write adjacent prompts that must not activate the skill.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Explicit Invocation
steps.instruction:
Use a changed theory case derived from this module: agent ext triggers. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

content.text: Support explicit invocation when deterministic use is required. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

steps.title: Build Explicit Invocation evidence
steps.instruction: Append a agent-ext-explicit-invocation-stfr1c: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Explicit Invocation into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
agent-ext-explicit-invocation-stfr1c:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

steps.title: Defend the Explicit Invocation model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Explicit Invocation applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Support explicit invocation when deterministic use is required.
